close_reason: Test fixture keys are intentionally external (not committed to repo for security). load_or_skip is the correct pattern for CI without key material.
description:
tests/load_or_skip macro (lib.rs:300) returns silently when /tmp/secretx-test-keys/*.der are absent. Test pattern is identical to t2qi.7 (keyring integration test silently no-ops without env var).

Risk: ed25519/p256/rsa-pss test bodies all skip if files are missing. CI without the openssl-genpkey setup step gets a green test run with effectively only 4 negative-path tests covering from_uri parsing.

Two options:
(a) generate keys in-process at test setup using ed25519_dalek::SigningKey::generate, p256::ecdsa::SigningKey::random, rsa::RsaPrivateKey::new — pulls in rand as a dev-dep, but tests run anywhere. Preferred.
(b) make the absence of fixture files a hard error rather than a silent skip; document the genpkey commands as a Makefile target.

Option (a) is the workspace direction; pkcs11 and aws-kms have similar setup-required tests but they NEED real backends, whereas this crate's tests do not need anything except in-memory keys.
title: Test fixture key files are external — load_or_skip silently no-ops in CI without setup
